Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>deep-research 以一个activity的启动为例子,详细说明窗口的添加过程,绘制过程,如何送到sf进行合成显示</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-18</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on durable technical signal:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)
- Concrete debugging or investigative findings from logs, traces, queue state, database rows, and code-path inspection

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored, discovered, confirmed, traced

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built or learned):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"
- "Observation queue for claude-mem session timed out waiting for an agent pool slot"
- "Fallback processing abandoned pending messages after Gemini and OpenRouter returned 404"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings with no follow-on finding
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- File related research that comes back empty or not found

If skipping, return an empty response only. Do not explain the skip in prose.

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision | security_alert | security_note ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

和Java 基础都有了提升,是时候该去看看 DecorView 的添加显示。 View 的绘制系列文章: Android View 绘制流程之 DecorView 与ViewRootImpl Android View 的绘制流程之 Measure 过程详解 (一) Android View 的绘制流程之 Layout 和Draw 过程详解 (二) Android View 的事件分发原理解析 Android 自定义 View 详解 概论 Android 中Activity 是作为应用程序的载体存在,代表着一个完整的用户界面,提供了一个窗口来绘制各种视图,当Activity 启动时,我们会通过 setContentView 方法来设置一个内容视图,这个内容视图就是用户看到的界面。 那么 View 和activity 是如何关联在一起的呢 ? 上图是 View 和Activity 之间的关系。先解释图中一些类的作用以及相关关系: Activity : 对于每一个 activity 都会有拥有一个 PhoneWindow。 PhoneWindow :该类继承于 Window 类,是Window 类的具体实现,即我们可以通过该类具体去绘制窗口。并且,该类内部包含了一个 DecorView 对象,该DectorView 对象是所有应用窗口的根 View。 DecorView 是一个应用窗口的根容器,它本质上是一个 FrameLayout。DecorView 有唯一一个子 View,它是一个垂直 LinearLayout,包含两个子元素,一个是 TitleView( ActionBar 的容器),另一个是 ContentView(窗口内容的容器)。 ContentView :是一个 FrameLayout(android.R.id.content),我们平常用的 setContentView 就是设置它的子 View 。 WindowManager : 是一个接口,里面常用的方法有:添加View,更新View和删除View。主要是用来管理 Window 的。WindowManager 具体的实现类是WindowManagerImpl。最终,Wind\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"date\\\\\\\": 不过最新推荐用tools/monitor.bat代替单独的hierarchyviewer.bat;hierarchyviewer.bat在工程目录下也存在prebuilts/devtools/tools 该篇主要介绍Activity中窗口的创建过程 以及 添加到WMS的过程。 第二部分 综述总结 ,先将上述两个过程的内容做了比较简洁的总结。 第三部分 Activity窗口添加过程 ,跟踪源码详细 LAST_APPLICATION_WINDOW。对应一个Activity,token需设置成Activity的token。 如:Activity。 子窗口(1000 ~ 1999): FIRST_SUB_WINDOW ~ LAST_SUB_WINDOW。必须要有一个父窗口,token需设置成父窗口的token。 如:PopupWindow,依附于Activity。 系统窗口(2000 ~ 2999): FIRST_SYSTEM_WINDOW ~ LAST_SYSTEM_WINDOW。系统级的 不需要对应Activity 也不需要有父窗口,应用进程一般没有权限创建,只有系统进程可以创建。如:上面列出了部分常见的系统窗口,状态栏、来电、toast、输入法等等。 几个重要类 下面几个类是后续经常看到的,这里主要看下他们直接的继承关系,后面看到比较容易理解。 public abstract      \\\\\\\"date\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"2016-12-22 14:40:29\\\\\\\"\\\\n    },\\\\n    {\\\\n      \\\\\\\"title\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Android View 绘制流程之 DecorView 与ViewRootImpl - huansky - 博客园\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"link\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"https://www.cnblogs.com/huansky/p/11911549.html\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"一年多以前,曾经以为自己对 View 的添加显示逻辑已经有所了解了,事后发现也只是懂了些皮毛而已。经过一年多的实战,Android 和Java 基础都有了提升,是时候该去看看 DecorView 的添加显示。 View 的绘制系列文章: Android View 绘制流程之 DecorView 与ViewRootImpl Android View 的绘制流程之 Measure 过程详解 (一) Android View 的绘制流程之 Layout 和Draw 过程详解 (二) Android View 的事件分发原理解析 Android 自定义 View 详解 概论 Android 中Activity 是作为应用程序的载体存在,代表着一个完整的用户界面,提供了一个窗口来绘制各种视图,当Activity 启动时,我们会通过 setContentView 方法来设置一个内容视图,这个内容视图就是用户看到的界面。 那么 View 和activity 是如何关联在一起的呢 ? 上图是 View 和Activity 之间的关系。先解释图中一些类的作用以及相关关系: Activity : 对于每一个 activity 都会有拥有一个 PhoneWindow。 PhoneWindow :该类继承于 Window 类,是Window 类的具体实现,即我们可以通过该类具体去绘制窗口。并且,该类内部包含了一个 DecorView 对象,该DectorView 对象是所有应用窗口的根 View。 DecorView 是一个应用窗口的根容器,它本质上是一个 FrameLayout。DecorView 有唯一一个子 View,它是一个垂直 LinearLayout,包含两个子元素,一个是 TitleView( ActionBar 的容器),另一个是 ContentView(窗口内容的容器)。 ContentView :是一个 FrameLayout(android.R.id.content),我们平常用的 setContentView 就是设置它的子 View 。 WindowManager : 是一个接口,里面常用的方法有:添加View,更新View和删除View。主要是用来管理 Window 的。WindowManager 具体的实现类是WindowManagerImpl。最终,Wind\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"date\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"https://www.cnblogs.com/fanglongxiang/p/13995868.html\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"前言 首先看一个Android界面的布局层次结构,最直观的看一下: 我们能清晰看到,这个界面分成了3部分:顶部状态栏(statusbar)、底部导航栏(navigationbar)、应用界面。 题外话: 查看布局的层次结构,工具或途径可以参考下面的。 Android Studio:Tools->Layout Inspector->选择要查看的进程; SDK Tools:tools/hierarchyviewer.bat。 不过最新推荐用tools/monitor.bat代替单独的hierarchyviewer.bat;hierarchyviewer.bat在工程目录下也存在prebuilts/devtools/tools 该篇主要介绍Activity中窗口的创建过程 以及 添加到WMS的过程。 第二部分 综述总结 ,先将上述两个过程的内容做了比较简洁的总结。 第三部分 Activity窗口添加过程 ,跟踪源码详细 讲述了 Activity窗口的创建过程 以及 添加到WMS过程。 由于第三部分 跟踪源码,这个过程比较长,涉及比较多,相对枯燥。所以把总结先放到了第二部分。这样,如果了解过源码或这个过程的,可以只看第二部分。没了解过的,也可以通过第二部分有个大概了TEM_WINDOW+13;} 应用窗口(1 ~ 99): FIRST_APPLICATION_WINDOW ~ LAST_APPLICATION_WINDOW。对应一个Activity,token需设置成Activity的token。 如:Activity。 子窗口(1000 ~ 1999): FIRST_SUB_WINDOW ~ LAST_SUB_WINDOW。必须要有一个父窗口,token需设置成父窗口的token。 如:PopupWindow,依附于Activity。 系统窗口(2000 ~ 2999): FIRST_SYSTEM_WINDOW ~ LAST_SYSTEM_WINDOW。系统级的 不需要对应Activity 也不需要有父窗口,应用进程一般没有权限创建,只有系统进程可以创建。如:上面列出了部分常见的系统窗口,状态栏、来电、toast、输入法等等。 几个重要类 下面几个类是后续经常看到的,这里主要看下他们直接的继承关系,后面看到比较容易理解。 public abstract <observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Bash</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-07-18T13:25:48.397Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>/home/cwtrocks/aosp</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"cd /tmp\\npython3 << 'PY'\\nimport urllib.request, base64\\nfiles = [\\n  (\\\"ViewRootImpl\\\",     \\\"core/java/android/view/ViewRootImpl.java\\\"),\\n  (\\\"WindowManagerService\\\", \\\"services/core/java/com/android/server/wm/WindowManagerService.java\\\"),\\n  (\\\"WindowManagerGlobal\\\", \\\"core/java/android/view/WindowManagerGlobal.java\\\"),\\n  (\\\"PhoneWindow\\\",      \\\"core/java/android/policy/PhoneWindow.java\\\"),\\n  (\\\"ActivityThread\\\",   \\\"core/java/android/app/ActivityThread.java\\\"),\\n  (\\\"Activity\\\",         \\\"core/java/android/app/Activity.java\\\"),\\n  (\\\"View\\\",             \\\"core/java/android/view/View.java\\\"),\\n  (\\\"WindowManagerImpl\\\",\\\"core/java/android/view/WindowManagerImpl.java\\\"),\\n]\\nfor fname, p in files:\\n  url = f\\\"https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/base/+/refs/heads/main/{p}?format=TEXT\\\"\\n  try:\\n    raw = urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=60).read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')\\n    # header ends with \\\"raw content:\\\" line\\n    idx = raw.find('raw content:')\\n    if idx < 0:\\n      print('NO HEADER:', fname)\\n      continue\\n    body = raw[idx + len('raw content:'):].strip()\\n    pad = '=' * (-len(body) % 4)\\n    decoded = base64.b64decode(body + pad).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')\\n    with open('/tmp/' + fname + '.java', 'w') as f:\\n      f.write(decoded)\\n    print(fname, 'lines:', decoded.count('\\\\n'), 'size:', len(decoded))\\n  except Exception as e:\\n    print('FAIL', fname, e)\\nPY\\nls -la /tmp/ViewRootImpl.java /tmp/WindowManagerService.java /tmp/WindowManagerGlobal.java /tmp/PhoneWindow.java /tmp/ActivityThread.java /tmp/Activity.java /tmp/View.java /tmp/WindowManagerImpl.java 2>&1 | head -10\",\"description\":\"Python urllib with proper header-strip\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"NO HEADER: ViewRootImpl\\nNO HEADER: WindowManagerService\\nNO HEADER: WindowManagerGlobal\\nFAIL PhoneWindow HTTP Error 404: Not Found\\nNO HEADER: ActivityThread\\nNO HEADER: Activity\\nNO HEADER: View\\nNO HEADER: WindowManagerImpl\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 0 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/Activity.java\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 0 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/ActivityThread.java\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 4 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/PhoneWindow.java\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 0 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/View.java\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 0 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/ViewRootImpl.java\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 0 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/WindowManagerGlobal.java\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 0 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/WindowManagerImpl.java\\n-rw-r--r-- 1 cwtrocks cwtrocks 0 Jul 18 21:25 /tmp/WindowManagerService.java\",\"stderr\":\"\\nShell cwd was reset to /home/cwtrocks/aosp\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

This automatically respects the clip/dirty region\\n            // or\\n            // If we are applying an offset, we need to clear the area\\n            // where the offset doesn't appear to avoid having garbage\\n            // left in the blank areas.\\n            if (!canvas.isOpaque() || yoff != 0 || xoff != 0) {\\n                canvas.drawColor(0, PorterDuff.Mode.CLEAR);\\n            }\\n\\n            dirty.setEmpty();\\n            mIsAnimating = false;\\n            mView.mPrivateFlags |= View.PFLAG_DRAWN;\\n\\n            if (DEBUG_DRAW) {\\n                Context cxt = mView.getContext();\\n                Log.i(mTag, \\\"Drawing: package:\\\" + cxt.getPackageName() +\\n                        \\\", metrics=\\\" + cxt.getResources().getDisplayMetrics() +\\n                        \\\", compatibilityInfo=\\\" + cxt.getResources().getCompatibilityInfo());\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"====== find performDraw exact line + view around =======\\n740:    /** The reaason the last call to performDraw() returned false */\\n4412:            if (!performDraw(mActiveSurfaceSyncGroup)) {\\n5383:    private boolean performDraw(@Nullable SurfaceSyncGroup surfaceSyncGroup) {\\n\\n====== 找 draw( 方法定义 =======\\n5601:    private boolean draw(boolean fullRedrawNeeded, @Nullable SurfaceSyncGroup activeSyncGroup,\\n5811:    private boolean drawSoftware(Surface surface, AttachInfo attachInfo, int xoff, int yoff,\\n\\n====== 提取行 5700-5850（performDraw + draw 入口区）======\\n\\n        boolean useAsyncReport = false;\\n        if (!dirty.isEmpty() || mIsAnimating || accessibilityFocusDirty) {\\n            if (isHardwareEnabled()) {\\n                // If accessibility focus moved, always invalidate the root.\\n                boolean invalidateRoot = accessibilityFocusDirty || mInvalidateRootRequested;\\n                mInvalidateRootRequested = false;\\n\\n                // Draw with hardware renderer.\\n                mIsAnimating = false;\\n\\n                if (mHardwareYOffset != yOffset || mHardwareXOffset != xOffset) {\\n                    mHardwareYOffset = yOffset;\\n                    mHardwareXOffset = xOffset;\\n                    invalidateRoot = true;\\n                }\\n\\n                if (invalidateRoot) {\\n                    mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.invalidateRoot();\\n                }\\n\\n                dirty.setEmpty();\\n\\n                // Stage the content drawn size now. It will be transferred to the renderer\\n                // shortly before the draw commands get send to the renderer.\\n                final boolean updated = updateContentDrawBounds();\\n\\n                if (mReportNextDraw) {\\n                    // report next draw overrides setStopped()\\n                    // This value is re-sync'd to the value of mStopped\\n                    // in the handling of mReportNextDraw post-draw.\\n                    mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.setStopped(false);\\n                }\\n\\n                if (updated) {\\n                    requestDrawWindow();\\n                }\\n\\n                useAsyncReport = true;\\n\\n                if (mHdrRenderState.updateForFrame(mAttachInfo.mDrawingTime)) {\\n                    final float renderRatio = mHdrRenderState.getRenderHdrSdrRatio();\\n                    applyTransactionOnDraw(mTransaction.setExtendedRangeBrightness(\\n                            getSurfaceControl(), renderRatio,\\n                            mHdrRenderState.getDesiredHdrSdrRatio()));\\n                    mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.setTargetHdrSdrRatio(renderRatio);\\n                }\\n\\n                if (activeSyncGroup != null) {\\n                    registerCallbacksForSync(syncBuffer, activeSyncGroup);\\n                    if (syncBuffer) {\\n                        mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.forceDrawNextFrame();\\n                    }\\n                } else if (mHasPendingTransactions) {\\n                    // Register a callback if there's no sync involved but there were calls to\\n                    // applyTransactionOnDraw. If there is a sync involved, the sync callback will\\n                    // handle merging the pending transaction.\\n                    registerCallbackForPendingTransactions();\\n                }\\n\\n                long timeNs = SystemClock.uptimeNanos();\\n                mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.draw(mView, mAttachInfo, this);\\n\\n                // Only trigger once per {@link ViewRootImpl} instance.\\n                if (mAppStartInfoTimestampsFlagValue && mRenderThreadDrawStartTimeNs == -1) {\\n                    mRenderThreadDrawStartTimeNs = timeNs;\\n                }\\n            } else {\\n                // If we get here with a disabled & requested hardware renderer, something went\\n                // wrong (an invalidate posted right before we destroyed the hardware surface\\n                // for instance) so we should just bail out. Locking the surface with software\\n                // rendering at this point would lock it forever and prevent hardware renderer\\n                // from doing its job when it comes back.\\n                // Before we request a new frame we must however attempt to reinitiliaze the\\n                // hardware renderer if it's in requested state. This would happen after an\\n                // eglTerminate() for instance.\\n                if (mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer != null &&\\n                        !mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.isEnabled() &&\\n                        mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.isRequested() &&\\n                        mSurface.isValid()) {\\n\\n                    try {\\n                        mAttachInfo.mThreadedRenderer.initializeIfNeeded(\\n                                mWidth, mHeight, mAttachInfo, mSurface, surfaceInsets);\\n                    } catch (OutOfResourcesException e) {\\n                        handleOutOfResourcesException(e);\\n                        return false;\\n                    }\\n\\n                    mFullRedrawNeeded = true;\\n                    scheduleTraversals();\\n                    return false;\\n                }\\n\\n                if (!drawSoftware(surface, mAttachInfo, xOffset, yOffset,\\n                        scalingRequired, dirty, surfaceInsets)) {\\n                    return false;\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        if (animating) {\\n            mFullRedrawNeeded = true;\\n            scheduleTraversals();\\n        }\\n        return useAsyncReport;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @return true if drawing was successful, false if an error occurred\\n     */\\n    private boolean drawSoftware(Surface surface, AttachInfo attachInfo, int xoff, int yoff,\\n            boolean scalingRequired, Rect dirty, Rect surfaceInsets) {\\n\\n        // Draw with software renderer.\\n        final Canvas canvas;\\n\\n        try {\\n            canvas = mSurface.lockCanvas(dirty);\\n            canvas.setDensity(mDensity);\\n        } catch (Surface.OutOfResourcesException e) {\\n            handleOutOfResourcesException(e);\\n            return false;\\n        } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\\n            Log.e(mTag, \\\"Could not lock surface\\\", e);\\n            // Don't assume this is due to out of memory, it could be\\n            // something else, and if it is something else then we could\\n            // kill stuff (or ourself) for no reason.\\n            mLayoutRequested = true;    // ask wm for a new surface next time.\\n            return false;\\n        }\\n\\n        try {\\n            if (DEBUG_ORIENTATION || DEBUG_DRAW) {\\n                Log.v(mTag, \\\"Surface \\\" + surface + \\\" drawing to bitmap w=\\\"\\n                        + canvas.getWidth() + \\\", h=\\\" + canvas.getHeight() + \\\", dirty: \\\" + dirty\\n                        + \\\", xOff=\\\" + xoff + \\\", yOff=\\\" + yoff);\\n                //canvas.drawARGB(255, 255, 0, 0);\\n            }\\n\\n            // If this bitmap's format includes an alpha channel, we\\n            // need to clear it before drawing so that the child will\\n            // properly re-composite its drawing on a transparent\\n            // background. This automatically respects the clip/dirty region\\n            // or\\n            // If we are applying an offset, we need to clear the area\\n            // where the offset doesn't appear to avoid having garbage\\n            // left in the blank areas.\\n            if (!canvas.isOpaque() || yoff != 0 || xoff != 0) {\\n                canvas.drawColor(0, PorterDuff.Mode.CLEAR);\\n            }\\n\\n            dirty.setEmpty();\\n            mIsAnimating = false;\\n            mView.mPrivateFlags |= View.PFLAG_DRAWN;\\n\\n            if (DEBUG_DRAW) {\\n                Context cxt = mView.getContext();\\n                Log.i(mTag, \\\"Drawing: package:\\\" + cxt.getPackageName() +\\n                        \\\", metrics=\\\" + cxt.getResources().getDisplayMetrics() +\\n                        \\\", compatibilityInfo=\\\" + cxt.getResources().getCompatibilityInfo());\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"====== Session class addToDisplayAsUser 完整 body（实际是 Session 子类）======\\n\\n\\n====== WMS.addWindow 关键 body (1533 行) =======\\n    public int addWindow(Session session, IWindow client, LayoutParams attrs, int viewVisibility,\\n            int displayId, int requestUserId, @InsetsType int requestedVisibleTypes,\\n            InputChannel outInputChannel, InsetsState outInsetsState,\\n            InsetsSourceControl.Array outActiveControls, Rect outAttachedFrame,\\n            float[] outSizeCompatScale) {\\n        outActiveControls.set(null, false /* copyControls */);\\n        int[] appOp = new int[1];\\n        final boolean isRoundedCornerOverlay = (attrs.privateFlags\\n                & PRIVATE_FLAG_IS_ROUNDED_CORNERS_OVERLAY) != 0;\\n        int res = mPolicy.checkAddPermission(attrs.type, isRoundedCornerOverlay, attrs.packageName,\\n                appOp, displayId);\\n        if (res != ADD_OKAY) {\\n            return res;\\n        }\\n\\n        WindowState parentWindow = null;\\n        final int callingUid = Binder.getCallingUid();\\n        final int callingPid = Binder.getCallingPid();\\n        final long origId = Binder.clearCallingIdentity();\\n        final int type = attrs.type;\\n\\n        synchronized (mGlobalLock) {\\n            if (!mDisplayReady) {\\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Display has not been initialialized\\\");\\n            }\\n            if (session.isClientDead()) {\\n                ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR, \\\"Attempted to add window with a client %s \\\"\\n                        + \\\"that is dead. Aborting.\\\", session);\\n                return WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_APP_EXITING;\\n            }\\n\\n            if (type >= FIRST_SUB_WINDOW && type <= LAST_SUB_WINDOW) {\\n                parentWindow = windowForClientLocked(null, attrs.token, false);\\n                if (parentWindow == null) {\\n                    ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR, \\\"Attempted to add window with token that is not a window: \\\"\\n                            + \\\"%s.  Aborting.\\\", attrs.token);\\n                    return WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_BAD_SUBWINDOW_TOKEN;\\n                }\\n                if (parentWindow.mAttrs.type >= FIRST_SUB_WINDOW\\n                        && parentWindow.mAttrs.type <= LAST_SUB_WINDOW) {\\n                    ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR, \\\"Attempted to add window with token that is a sub-window: \\\"\\n                            + \\\"%s.  Aborting.\\\", attrs.token);\\n                    return WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_BAD_SUBWINDOW_TOKEN;\\n                }\\n            }\\n            final DisplayContent displayContent = parentWindow != null\\n                    ? parentWindow.mDisplayContent\\n                    : getDisplayContentOrCreate(displayId, attrs.token);\\n\\n            if (displayContent == null) {\\n                ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR, \\\"Attempted to add window to a display that does \\\"\\n                        + \\\"not exist: %d. Aborting.\\\", displayId);\\n                return WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_INVALID_DISPLAY;\\n            }\\n            if (!displayContent.hasAccess(session.mUid)) {\\n                ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR,\\n                        \\\"Attempted to add window to a display for which the application \\\"\\n                                + \\\"does not have access: %d.  Aborting.\\\",\\n                        displayContent.getDisplayId());\\n                return WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_INVALID_DISPLAY;\\n            }\\n\\n            if (mWindowMap.containsKey(client.asBinder())) {\\n                ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR, \\\"Window %s is already added\\\", client);\\n                return WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_DUPLICATE_ADD;\\n            }\\n\\n            if (type == TYPE_PRESENTATION || type == TYPE_PRIVATE_PRESENTATION) {\\n                mDisplayManagerInternal.onPresentation(displayContent.getDisplay().getDisplayId(),\\n                        /*isShown=*/ true);\\n            }\\n\\n            if (type == TYPE_PRIVATE_PRESENTATION && !displayContent.isPrivate()) {\\n                ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR,\\n                        \\\"Attempted to add private presentation window to a non-private display.  \\\"\\n                                + \\\"Aborting.\\\");\\n                return WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_PERMISSION_DENIED;\\n            }\\n\\n            if (type == TYPE_PRESENTATION && !displayContent.getDisplay().isPublicPresentation()) {\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>

  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"====== addWindow 后续：attachToDisplayContent + addToToken + performSurfacePlacement =======\\n            }\\n\\n            if (imMayMove) {\\n                displayContent.computeImeTarget(true /* updateImeTarget */);\\n                if (win.isImeOverlayLayeringTarget()) {\\n                    dispatchImeTargetOverlayVisibilityChanged(client.asBinder(), win.mAttrs.type,\\n                            win.isVisibleRequestedOrAdding(), false /* removed */,\\n                            displayContent.getDisplayId());\\n                }\\n            }\\n\\n            // Don't do layout here, the window must call\\n            // relayout to be displayed, so we'll do it there.\\n            if (win.mActivityRecord != null && win.mActivityRecord.isEmbedded()) {\\n                // Assign child layers from the parent Task if the Activity is embedded.\\n                win.getTask().assignChildLayers();\\n            } else {\\n                win.getParent().assignChildLayers();\\n            }\\n\\n            if (focusChanged) {\\n                displayContent.getInputMonitor().setInputFocusLw(displayContent.mCurrentFocus,\\n                        false /*updateInputWindows*/);\\n            }\\n            displayContent.getInputMonitor().updateInputWindowsLw(false /*force*/);\\n\\n            ProtoLog.v(WM_DEBUG_ADD_REMOVE, \\\"addWindow: New client %s\\\"\\n                    + \\\": window=%s Callers=%s\\\", client.asBinder(), win, Debug.getCallers(5));\\n\\n            boolean needToSendNewConfiguration =\\n                    win.isVisibleRequestedOrAdding() && displayContent.updateOrientation();\\n            if (win.providesDisplayDecorInsets()) {\\n                needToSendNewConfiguration |= displayPolicy.updateDecorInsetsInfo();\\n            }\\n            if (needToSendNewConfiguration) {\\n                displayContent.sendNewConfiguration();\\n            }\\n\\n            // This window doesn't have a frame yet. Don't let this window cause the insets change.\\n            displayContent.getInsetsStateController().updateAboveInsetsState(\\n                    false /* notifyInsetsChanged */);\\n\\n            win.fillInsetsState(outInsetsState, true /* copySources */);\\n            getInsetsSourceControls(win, outActiveControls);\\n\\n            if (win.mLayoutAttached) {\\n                outAttachedFrame.set(win.getParentWindow().getFrame());\\n                if (win.mInvGlobalScale != 1f) {\\n                    outAttachedFrame.scale(win.mInvGlobalScale);\\n                }\\n            } else {\\n                // Make this invalid which indicates a null attached frame.\\n                outAttachedFrame.set(0, 0, -1, -1);\\n            }\\n            outSizeCompatScale[0] = win.getCompatScaleForClient();\\n        }\\n\\n        Binder.restoreCallingIdentity(origId);\\n\\n        return res;\\n    }\\n\\n    private boolean unprivilegedAppCanCreateTokenWith(WindowState parentWindow,\\n            int callingUid, int type, int rootType, IBinder tokenForLog, String packageName) {\\n        if (rootType >= FIRST_APPLICATION_WINDOW && rootType <= LAST_APPLICATION_WINDOW) {\\n            ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR, \\\"Attempted to add application window with unknown token \\\"\\n                    + \\\"%s.  Aborting.\\\", tokenForLog);\\n            return false;\\n        }\\n        if (rootType == TYPE_INPUT_METHOD) {\\n            ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR, \\\"Attempted to add input method window with unknown token \\\"\\n                    + \\\"%s.  Aborting.\\\", tokenForLog);\\n            return false;\\n        }\\n        if (rootType == TYPE_VOICE_INTERACTION) {\\n            ProtoLog.w(WM_ERROR,\\n                    \\\"Attempted to add voice interaction window with unknown token \\\"\\n                            + \\\"%s.  Aborting.\\\", tokenForLog);\\n            return false;\\n        }\\n\\n====== WindowSurfacePlacer.performSurfacePlacement 关键 =======\\n434:    /** Caller will performSurfacePlacement */\\n2137:            mWindowPlacerLocked.performSurfacePlacement();\\n2221:                    mWindowPlacerLocked.performSurfacePlacement();\\n2508:            // This must be called before the call to performSurfacePlacement.\\n2547:            mWindowPlacerLocked.performSurfacePlacement(true /* force */);\\n3813:            mWindowPlacerLocked.performSurfacePlacement();\\n4664:                            \\\"updateRotation: performSurfacePlacement\\\");\\n4665:                    mWindowPlacerLocked.performSurfacePlacement();\\n5908:                            mWindowPlacerLocked.performSurfacePlacement();\\n5939:                        mWindowPlacerLocked.performSurfacePlacement();\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>

startingWindow=%s\\\",\\n                        activity, win);\\n            } else if (type == TYPE_INPUT_METHOD\\n                    // IME window is always touchable.\\n                    // Ignore non-touchable windows e.g. Stylus InkWindow.java.\\n                    && (win.getAttrs().flags & FLAG_NOT_TOUCHABLE) == 0) {\\n                displayContent.setInputMethodWindowLocked(win);\\n                imMayMove = false;\\n            } else if (type == TYPE_INPUT_METHOD_DIALOG) {\\n                displayContent.computeImeTarget(true /* updateImeTarget */);\\n                imMayMove = false;\\n            } else {\\n                if (type == TYPE_WALLPAPER) {\\n                    displayContent.mWallpaperController.clearLastWallpaperTimeoutTime();\\n                    displayContent.pendingLayoutChanges |= FINISH_LAYOUT_REDO_WALLPAPER;\\n                } else if (win.hasWallpaper()) {\\n                    displayContent.pendingLayoutChanges |= FINISH_LAYOUT_REDO_WALLPAPER;\\n                } else if (displayContent.mWallpaperController.isBelowWallpaperTarget(win)) {\\n                    // If there is currently a wallpaper being shown, and\\n                    // the base layer of the new window is below the current\\n                    // layer of the target window, then adjust the wallpaper.\\n                    // This is to avoid a new window being placed between the\\n                    // wallpaper and its target.\\n                    displayContent.pendingLayoutChanges |= FINISH_LAYOUT_REDO_WALLPAPER;\\n                }\\n            }\\n\\n            final WindowStateAnimator winAnimator = win.mWinAnimator;\\n            winAnimator.mEnterAnimationPending = true;\\n            winAnimator.mEnteringAnimation = true;\\n\\n            if (displayPolicy.areSystemBarsForcedConsumedLw()) {\\n                res |= WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_FLAG_ALWAYS_CONSUME_SYSTEM_BARS;\\n            }\\n            if (displayContent.isInTouchMode()) {\\n                res |= WindowManagerGlobal.ADD_FLAG_IN_TOUCH_MODE;\\n            }\\n            if (win.mActivityRecord == null || win.mActivityRecord.isClientVisible()) {\\n                res cookie\\\");\\n                } else {\\n                    targetTask = targetActivity.getTask();\\n                    taskWindowContainerToken = to\\n        // the launched activity being closed, but the launched task is still open, so now attempt\\n        // to look for the task directly.\\n        if (taskWindowContainerToken == null && session.getTaskId() != -1) {\\n            int targetTaskId = session.getTaskId();\\n            targetTask = mRoot.getTask(task -> task.isTaskId(targetTaskId));\\n            if (targetTask == null) {\\n                Slog.w(TAG, \\\"Unable to find the task for this projection\\\");\\n            } else {\\n                taskWindowContainerToken = targetTask.mRemoteToken.toWindowContainerToken();\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        // If we were unable to find the launched task in either fashion, then something must have\\n        // wrong (i.e. the task was closed before capture started).\\n        if (taskWindowContainerToken == null) {\\n            Slog.w(TAG, \\\"Unable to find the WindowContainerToken for ContentRecordingSession\\\");\\n            return null;\\n        }\\n        return new WindowContainerInfo(targetTask.effectiveUid, taskWindowContainerToken);\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * You need ALLOW_SLIPPERY_TOUCHES permission to be able to set FLAG_SLIPPERY.\\n     */\\n    private int sanitizeFlagSlippery(int flags, String windowName, int callingUid, int callingPid) {\\n        if ((flags from '\\\" + windowName\\n                    + \\\"' because it doesn't have ALLOW_SLIPPERY_TOUCHES permission\\\");\\n            return flags & ~FLAG_SLIPPERY;\\n        }\\n        return Bash 2026-07-18T13:30:31.208Z /home/cwtrocks/aosp "{\"command\":\"echo '====== HardwareRenderer.createRenderNode + recordDraw (nSyncRt 路径) ======='\\nsed -n '430,500p' /tmp/HardwareRenderer.java\\necho\\necho '====== 找 Record Draw / nSyncRt ======='\\ngrep -nE \\\"nSyncRt|Record Draw\\\" /tmp/HardwareRenderer.java | head -5\",\"description\":\"HardwareRenderer sync/record draw\"}" "{\"stdout\":\"====== HardwareRenderer.createRenderNode + recordDraw (nSyncRt 路径) =======\\n * recommended value is false, as blocking for present will prevent pipelining from\\n * happening, reducing overall throughput. This is useful for situations such as\\n * {@link SurfaceHolder.Callback2#surfaceRedrawNeeded(SurfaceHolder)} where it is desired\\n * to block until a frame has been presented to ensure first-frame consistency with\\n * other Surfaces.\\n *\\n * @param shouldWait If true the next call to {@link #syncAndDraw()} will block until\\n * completion.\\n * @return this instance\\n */\\n public @NonNull FrameRenderRequest setWaitForPresent(boolean shouldWait) {\\n mWaitForPresent = shouldWait;\\n return this;\\n }\\n\\n /**\\n * Syncs the RenderNode tree to the render thread and requests a frame to be drawn. This\\n * {@link FrameRenderRequest} instance should no longer be used after calling this method.\\n * The system internally may reuse instances of {@link FrameRenderRequest} to reduce\\n * allocation churn.\\n *\\n * @return The result of the sync operation.\\n */\\n @SyncAndDrawResult\\n public int syncAndDraw() {\\n int syncResult = syncAndDrawFrame(mFrameInfo);\\n if (mWaitForPresent && (syncResult & SYNC_FRAME_DROPPED) == 0) {\\n fence();\\n }\\n return syncResult;\\n }\\n }\\n\\n private FrameRenderRequest mRenderRequest = new FrameRenderRequest();\\n\\n /**\\n * Returns a {@link FrameRenderRequest} that can be used to render a new frame. This is used\\n * to synchronize the RenderNode content provided by {@link #setContentRoot(RenderNode)} with\\n * the RenderThread and then renders a single frame to the Surface set with\\n * {@link #setSurface(Surface)}.\\n *\\n * @return An instance of {@link FrameRenderRequest}. The instance may be reused for every\\n * frame, so the caller should not hold onto it for longer than a single render request.\\n */\\n public @NonNull FrameRenderRequest createRenderRequest() {\\n mRenderRequest.reset();\\n return mRenderRequest;\\n }\\n\\n /**\\n * Syncs the RenderNode tree to the render thread and requests a frame to be drawn.\\n *\\n * @hide\\n */\\n @SyncAndDrawResult\\n public int syncAndDrawFrame(@NonNull FrameInfo frameInfo) {\\n return nSyncAndDrawFrame(mNativeProxy, frameInfo.frameInfo, frameInfo.frameInfo.length);\\n }\\n\\n /**\\n * Suspends any current rendering into the surface but do not do any destruction. This\\n * is useful to temporarily suspend using the active Surface in order to do any Surface\\n * mutations necessary.\\n *\\n *

Any subsequent draws will override the pause, resuming normal operation.\\n *\\n * @return true if there was an outstanding render request, false otherwise. If this is true\\n * the caller should ensure that {@link #createRenderRequest()}\\n * and {@link FrameRenderRequest#syncAndDraw()} is called at the soonest\\n * possible time to resume normal operation.\\n *\\n\\n====== 找 Record Draw / nSyncRt =======\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}" If a or block above contains an "" marker, that field was truncated to fit the observer's context window. This is useful for situations such as\\n * {@link SurfaceHolder.Callback2#surfaceRedrawNeeded(SurfaceHolder)} where it is desired\\n * to block until a frame has been presented to ensure first-frame consistency with\\n * other Surfaces.\\n *\\n * @param shouldWait If true the next call to {@link #syncAndDraw()} will block until\\n * completion.\\n * @return this instance\\n */\\n public @NonNull FrameRenderRequest setWaitForPresent(boolean shouldWait) {\\n mWaitForPresent = shouldWait;\\n return this;\\n }\\n\\n /**\\n * Syncs the RenderNode tree to the render thread and requests a frame to be drawn. This\\n * {@link FrameRenderRequest} instance should no longer be used after calling this method.\\n * The system internally may reuse instances of {@link FrameRenderRequest} to reduce\\n * allocation churn.\\n *\\n * @return The result of the sync operation.\\n */\\n @SyncAndDrawResult\\n public int syncAndDraw() {\\n int syncResult = syncAndDrawFrame(mFrameInfo);\\n if (mWaitForPresent && (syncResult & SYNC_FRAME_DROPPED) == 0) {\\n fence();\\n }\\n return syncResult;\\n }\\n }\\n\\n private FrameRenderRequest mRenderRequest = new FrameRenderRequest();\\n\\n /**\\n * Returns a {@link FrameRenderRequest} that can be used to render a new frame. This is used\\n * to synchronize the RenderNode content provided by {@link #setContentRoot(RenderNode)} with\\n * the RenderThread and then renders a single frame to the Surface set with\\n * {@link #setSurface(Surface)}.\\n *\\n * @return An instance of {@link FrameRenderRequest}. The instance may be reused for every\\n * frame, so the caller should not hold onto it for longer than a single render request.\\n */\\n public @NonNull FrameRenderRequest createRenderRequest() {\\n mRenderRequest.reset();\\n return mRenderRequest;\\n }\\n\\n /**\\n * Syncs the RenderNode tree to the render thread and requests a frame to be drawn.\\n *\\n * @hide\\n */\\n @SyncAndDrawResult\\n public int syncAndDrawFrame(@NonNull FrameInfo frameInfo) {\\n return nSyncAndDrawFrame(mNativeProxy, frameInfo.frameInfo, frameInfo.frameInfo.length);\\n }\\n\\n /**\\n * Suspends any current rendering into the surface but do not do any destruction. This\\n * is useful to temporarily suspend using the active Surface in order to do any Surface\\n * mutations necessary.\\n *\\n *

Any subsequent draws will override the pause, resuming normal operation.\\n *\\n * @return true if there was an outstanding render request, false otherwise. If this is true\\n * the caller should ensure that {@link #createRenderRequest()}\\n * and {@link FrameRenderRequest#syncAndDraw()} is called at the soonest\\n * possible time to resume normal operation.\\n *\\n\\n====== 找 Record Draw / nSyncRt =======\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}" If a or block above contains an "" marker, that field was truncated to fit the observer's context window.
